name: python-observability-patterns
description: Observability patterns for Python applications. Triggers on: logging, metrics, tracing, opentelemetry, prometheus, observability, monitoring, structlog, correlation id.
capabilities: Configure structlog for production JSON logging
Create Prometheus counters, histograms, and gauges
Add request context and correlation IDs to logs
Build FastAPI middleware for metrics and tracing
Link traces to structured logs automatically
author: 0xDarkMatter
securitySummary: This is a documentation-only skill containing observability patterns for Python. All static findings are FALSE POSITIVES. The scanner misinterprets Python import statements as shell commands, localhost URLs as suspicious network connections, and a security filter definition as credential access. The previous audit correctly rated this as safe with no executable code, network calls, file system access, or external commands.
valueStatement: Python applications often lack clear logging, metrics, and tracing, making debugging difficult in production. This skill provides ready-to-use patterns for structlog, Prometheus metrics, and OpenTelemetry distributed tracing.
limitations: Does not include pre-built monitoring dashboards
Does not configure external observability backends

useCases.title: Debug Production Issues
useCases.description: Add correlation IDs and tracing to quickly identify root cause of errors in distributed systems.

useCases.title: Monitor Service Health
useCases.description: Expose Prometheus metrics for dashboards and alerting on request rates, latency, and error counts.

useCases.title: Track Request Flows
useCases.description: Implement distributed tracing to visualize how requests flow across microservices.

promptTemplates.title: Basic Logging Setup
promptTemplates.scenario: Add structured logging to Python app
promptTemplates.prompt: Show me how to configure structlog for production with JSON output and request context binding.

promptTemplates.title: HTTP Metrics
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a FastAPI middleware that records request count, latency histogram, and active connections as Prometheus metrics.

promptTemplates.title: Distributed Tracing
promptTemplates.prompt: Set up OpenTelemetry tracing with context propagation for a Python service that calls external APIs.

promptTemplates.title: Custom Span Decorator
promptTemplates.prompt: Write a Python decorator that wraps functions with OpenTelemetry spans and records exceptions as span events.

bestPractices: Use semantic event names (e.g., 'user_created' not 'log_message_123') for structured logs
Include request_id and user_id in all log entries for debugging traceability
Filter sensitive data (passwords, tokens, API keys) before logging with a processor
antiPatterns: Logging sensitive data like passwords, tokens, or PII in plain text
Using high cardinality labels (user_id, request_id) on Prometheus metrics
Missing error context in exception logging without error type and stack trace

faq.question: Which Python versions are supported?
faq.answer: Python 3.10 and newer. Requires structlog, opentelemetry-api, and prometheus-client packages.

faq.question: What observability backends work with these patterns?
faq.answer: OpenTelemetry exporters support Jaeger, Tempo, Datadog, and cloud solutions. Prometheus metrics work with any Prometheus-compatible scrape target.

faq.question: Can these patterns integrate with FastAPI?
faq.answer: Yes. FastAPI instrumentation packages from OpenTelemetry work alongside the middleware patterns shown for request context and metrics.

faq.question: Is data sent to external services?
faq.answer: No. The skill only provides code patterns. You configure and control all network endpoints for exporting logs and traces.

faq.question: Why are my metrics showing high cardinality?
faq.answer: Avoid labels with many unique values like user_id or request_id. Use labels like method, endpoint, or status instead.

faq.question: How does this compare to other logging libraries?
faq.answer: structlog provides structured logging with processors. Prometheus-client is metrics-focused. OpenTelemetry gives distributed tracing. They complement each other.
